title: Quick start and CLI surface docs still point to removed `init` / `scan` / `hooks install` commands
description: README quick start still tells users to run `pnpm dlx @fenglimg/fabric-cli init` at README.md:199, while docs/surfaces.md:24 still lists `scan` and `hooks install` as CLI examples, and docs/CODEBASE_LANDSCAPE.md:9,57,61 still says the public surface is `install/scan/doctor/uninstall` and cites `src/commands/init.ts` / `scan.ts`. The live registry in packages/cli/src/commands/index.ts:8-30 has no `init`, no `scan`, and no `hooks` subcommand; it now exposes `install`, `store`, `sync`, `info`, deprecated `whoami/status/scope-explain`, `doctor`, `uninstall`, `config`, hidden `plan-context-hint` / `onboard-coverage`, and `metrics`. `Test-Path` also shows `packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts` and `packages/cli/src/commands/scan.ts` do not exist. This means the primary onboarding docs tell users to invoke commands that the shipped CLI no longer provides.
fix_direction: Rewrite README / surfaces / landscape to match the current registry: use `fabric install` as the entrypoint, document that `fabric scan` was removed in favor of `fabric doctor --rescan`, remove `hooks install`, and surface the current `store` / `sync` / `info` / `metrics` commands where relevant.

title: MCP architecture and contract docs still describe deleted `fab_get_rule_sections` / `get-rules.ts` surfaces
description: docs/mcp-contracts.md:3,16,21,107 still says Fabric has only four MCP tools and documents `fab_get_rule_sections`; docs/ARCHITECTURE_DECISIONS.md:19,34-36,52-53 still points readers at `packages/server/src/tools/get-rules.ts` and `packages/server/src/services/get-rules.ts`, and says the editor loop is `fab_plan_context` + `fab_get_rule_sections`. In the live server, packages/server/src/index.ts:17-22 and 218-223 import/register `registerPlanContext`, `registerKnowledgeSections`, `registerRecall`, `registerArchiveScan`, `registerExtractKnowledge`, and `registerReview` — six tools total, with `fab_get_knowledge_sections` replacing the old name and additive `fab_recall` / `fab_archive_scan` already live. `Test-Path` confirms the referenced `get-rules.ts` files no longer exist. Integrators following the docs will target wrong tool names and stale implementation paths.
fix_direction: Update contracts and ADRs to the current MCP surface: rename `fab_get_rule_sections` to `fab_get_knowledge_sections`, add `fab_recall` and `fab_archive_scan`, and replace deleted `get-rules.ts` references with the current `plan-context` / `knowledge-sections` modules.

title: Test-seed and testing docs still validate a retired CLI surface (`scan` / `serve`, six-command registry)
description: The doc-driven test seed has not kept up with the current command surface. docs/testing-architecture.md:88 says the CLI help drift gate snapshots `fabric scan --help`; docs/test-seed/cli.md:13 still treats `serve` as a live command, line 73 says `fabric scan` is retired but the same file still anchors expectations around it, and lines 78-81 claim the registry has only six entries (`install,doctor,serve,uninstall,config,plan-context-hint`) plus `scan.ts`. The actual registry at packages/cli/src/commands/index.ts:8-30 has no `serve` or `scan` and now includes many additional commands (`store`, `sync`, `info`, `whoami`, `status`, `scope-explain`, `onboard-coverage`, `metrics`). This means the seed docs and test-architecture narrative keep propagating an obsolete command model to reviewers and maintainers.
fix_direction: Refresh `docs/test-seed/cli.md` and `docs/testing-architecture.md` so the documented drift gate and seed surface match the current registry, or explicitly mark them as historical if they are intentionally frozen.
